news:news.news:title: US President Trump has delayed escalation in the Iran conflict while various global issues, including domestic worker rights in Indonesia and Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws, are in focus.
news:news.news:keywords: US-Iran diplomatic relations, Colombian mercenaries in Sudan, Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws, domestic workers rights in Indonesia, mass trial in El Salvador

news:news.news:title: Kevin Warsh's nomination as Fed chair faces scrutiny amid political tensions, while Apple prepares for leadership change and gig-work apps push for healthcare deregulation.
news:news.news:keywords: Federal Reserve chair nomination, Apple leadership change, gig economy in healthcare, Purdue Pharma settlement, oil prices and market impact

news:news.news:title: SpaceX is considering a $60 billion acquisition of Cursor, while AI backlash grows ahead of upcoming elections and OpenAI enhances its image generator capabilities.
news:news.news:keywords: SpaceX acquisition news, AI impact on elections, OpenAI image generator update, Framework Laptop 13 Pro launch, Tim Cook's legacy at Apple

news:news.news:title: Victor Wembanyama's concussion raises concerns for the Spurs as they face playoff challenges, while the Mets extend their losing streak to 12 games.
news:news.news:keywords: NBA playoff updates, MLB losing streaks, NHL playoff performances, college sports controversies, soccer relegation news

news:news.news:title: Recent scientific discoveries include a new state of matter in distant planets, advanced imaging techniques, and breakthroughs in environmental and health applications.
news:news.news:keywords: new state of matter in planets, advanced imaging technology, environmental science breakthroughs, healthcare innovations, sustainable energy solutions

news:news.news:title: Tensions rise in the Middle East as the US maintains its blockade on Iran, while global incidents prompt security concerns in various regions.
news:news.news:keywords: US-Iran relations and peace talks, Apple CEO transition, security incidents in Mexico, earthquake risks in Japan, IS-linked group rescues in DR Congo

news:news.news:title: Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus set to take over in September, amid rising oil prices and ongoing economic concerns.
news:news.news:keywords: Apple leadership change, US tariff refunds, impact of oil prices on markets, Uber and Lyft driver challenges, US population crisis concerns

news:news.news:title: Tim Cook is stepping down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus set to take over in September, while Dyson launches a new travel-size hairdryer.
news:news.news:keywords: Apple leadership changes, Dyson new product launch, WhatsApp subscription service, Amazon price-fixing allegations, Silicon Valley consumer disconnect

news:news.news:title: The NBA playoffs continue with notable performances, while MLB faces injury setbacks and a significant rule controversy.
news:news.news:keywords: NBA playoffs updates, MLB injury news, PGA Tour schedule changes, NFL draft predictions, Victor Wembanyama DPOY award

news:news.news:title: Recent scientific advancements include a new termite control method, breakthroughs in dolomite growth, and innovative solutions for microplastic removal from water.
news:news.news:keywords: pest control innovations, geological breakthroughs, microplastic removal solutions, bacterial DNA research, sustainable energy technologies

news:news.news:title: Iran Faces Mass Job Losses Amid Conflict; SpaceX Eyes $60B Cursor Deal
Tim Cook Abruptly Steps Down as Apple CEO, John Ternus Takes the Helm Amid Economic Turmoil
